label: Immunity to Change
intent: User has a pattern that resists willpower — they know what they should do and still don't do it
trigger_signals: why do I keep doing this
protocol: Name the improvement goal: what does the user genuinely want to change?
Surface counter-behaviours: what do they actually do instead, even when they know better?
Uncover the hidden commitment: what does the counter-behaviour protect? Ask 'what would be the downside of fully committing to the goal?'
Surface the big assumption: 'If I gave up [hidden commitment], I assume that...'
Design a small, safe-to-fail test of the big assumption
opening_moves: What's the thing you keep meaning to do but don't?
When you don't follow through, what do you tend to do instead?
deepening_questions: What would be the downside of fully succeeding at that goal?
What are you protecting by NOT doing the thing?
If you imagine letting go of that protection — what feels scary?
What assumption would have to be wrong for the change to feel safe?
do_not: Do not reframe the counter-behaviour as laziness or weakness
Do not suggest the hidden commitment is irrational
Do not propose action plans before the big assumption is surfaced
exit_criteria: The user names a big assumption they hold that makes the change feel unsafe, and identifies one small test of that assumption
